# sim.Simulation { #tspaint.sim.Simulation }

```python
tspaint.sim.Simulation(ts, queries, labels, truth_states, sample_sets)
```

A simulated admixture with known truth — the return of :func:`simulate_admixture`.

Everything downstream needs (queries to paint, reference labels, ground-truth ancestry) is
derived from the demography's own population **roles** (see :func:`admixture_demography`), so no
caller ever needs the population-name constants.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**ts**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The simulated tree sequence — **with mutations** when ``mutation_rate`` was given.

<code>[**queries**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Query (admixed) haplotype node ids.

<code>[**labels**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Default reference panel: reference haplotype node id → ancestry-state index (``0`` / ``1``), ready to pass to :func:`tspaint.paint`.

<code>[**truth_states**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`), [int](`int`)\]\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per query, the true local-ancestry tracts ``(left, right, state)`` from the census — ready to score against a painting (e.g. :func:`tspaint.balanced_accuracy`).

<code>[**sample_sets**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [list](`list`)\[[int](`int`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Population name → its sample node ids, for building custom reference panels (e.g. proxy-only vs direct-source labels).
